title: Pointers, arrays and arithmetic deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Pointers, arrays and arithmetic; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Pointers, arrays and arithmetic: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Pointer arithmetic counts bytes for every pointer type and can traverse freely between separately allocated objects.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Pointer arithmetic is scaled by the pointed-to type and is defined only within one array object plus its one-past position.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Pointer arithmetic is scaled by the pointed-to type and is defined only within one array object plus its one-past position.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Pointer arithmetic is scaled by the pointed-to type and is defined only within one array object plus its one-past position.
Pointer arithmetic counts bytes for every pointer type and can traverse freely between separately allocated objects.

answer.explanation: Pointer arithmetic is scaled by the pointed-to type and is defined only within one array object plus its one-past position.

items.title: Pointers, arrays and arithmetic: complete the language rule

items.title: Pointers, arrays and arithmetic: executable application
items.prompt: Read up to 100 integers and sum them by advancing a pointer from the first element to the one-past endpoint without dereferencing the endpoint.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: pointer traversal confined to one array object

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises pointer traversal confined to one array object under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
